description_long: This kiosk features a modern, open-plan design with multiple partition walls arranged on a polygonal base, creating semi-enclosed display and interaction zones. The structure is predominantly white, with several large, flat panels that serve as display surfaces for images or information, some of which are mounted vertically and others horizontally. The central area is open, allowing for easy movement and access from multiple directions, while the perimeter panels provide visual separation and space for signage or exhibits. The kiosk lacks a traditional roof, instead utilizing the vertical panels to define its spatial boundaries. There is a prominent overhead sign with the word 'PLOY' in bold, three-dimensional letters, suspended above the kiosk. The overall aesthetic is minimalist and contemporary, with clean lines and a focus on functionality and visibility.
description: A modern, open-sided kiosk with white partition walls, display panels, and a bold overhead 'PLOY' sign, arranged on a polygonal base. The design is minimalist and functional, with ample space for movement and visual displays.
description_view_0: From this angle, the kiosk's open central area and the arrangement of display panels along the perimeter are clearly visible, with the 'PLOY' sign overhead.
description_view_1: This view highlights the rear and side panels, showing the layered arrangement of display surfaces and the open access points.
description_view_2: Here, the focus is on the back of the kiosk, revealing the multi-panel display wall and the unobstructed central space.
description_view_3: This perspective emphasizes the side and front display panels, as well as the open entry points and the overhead signage.
attribute_signage_type: Three-dimensional printed sign with the text 'PLOY', suspended above the kiosk's center.
attribute_roof_design: No traditional roof; open-top with vertical panels and an overhead sign as the main feature.
